title: Opinion | The best education for future success might surprise you
summary: Washington Post opinion on why liberal arts education remains valuable in AI era

reporting_brief.WHO: Liberal arts institutions (Roanoke College, Eastern CT, U Chicago), opinion leaders, educators
reporting_brief.WHAT: Argument that liberal arts (humanities, social sciences, natural sciences) become MORE valuable, not obsolete, in AI age; AI best used to enhance human judgment; liberal arts train critical evaluation skills
reporting_brief.WHY: As AI handles routine content generation and technical tasks, human skills in judgment, ethics, creativity, bias detection become differentiators; renaissance opportunity for humanities
reporting_brief.CONTEXT: Counternarrative to 'AI makes degrees obsolete'; shift in liberal arts positioning from luxury to necessity; institutions integrating AI into general ed as *analytical tool*, not replacement

SOURCES.title: Washington Post Opinion - Liberal Arts & AI
Higher Ed Dive - Liberal Arts & AI Integration
Eastern CT - Case for Liberal Arts in Age of AI

PERSPECTIVES.liberal_arts_institutions: Opportunity to reposition humanities as essential for AI literacy and judgment training
PERSPECTIVES.education_analysts: Skills in bias detection, evaluation, ethics increasingly valuable as AI becomes commodity
PERSPECTIVES.tech_perspective: AI literacy requires understanding sources, algorithms, context - inherently humanistic skills

reporting_brief.SUGGESTED_ANGLE: Liberal arts gets an AI upgrade: Why humanities may be the secret weapon for navigating AI futures
